title.purpose: Concise page or recovery headings where the user needs orientation.

menu.purpose: Task titles, controls, navigation, and compact data.

label.purpose: Section labels, capacity metadata, and small status text.

shadows.name: transient-menu
shadows.purpose: Separate select and popover content from the workspace.

shadows.name: static-content
shadows.purpose: Keep task rows, settings groups, and capacity summaries flat at rest.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Communicate hover, focus, selection, and feedback without delaying task work.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Remove transition choreography when prefers-reduced-motion is enabled.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single clear in-flow commitment action.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; height: 32px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 10px; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); font: 500 0.8125rem/1.125rem "Avenir Next", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif; transition: background-color 150ms ease-out, transform 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 80%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); outline-offset: 0; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: A quiet secondary action for compact surfaces.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; height: 32px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); font: 500 0.8125rem/1.125rem "Avenir Next", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif; transition: background-color 150ms ease-out, color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--muted); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); outline-offset: 0; }

components.name: Compact Input
components.description: The persistent capture field and compact settings control.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" aria-label="New task" placeholder="Add a task" />
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 32px; min-width: 0; padding: 4px 10px; border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: 10px; background: transparent; color: var(--foreground); font: 400 0.8125rem/1.125rem "Avenir Next", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: var(--ring); outline: 3px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Context Navigation
components.html: TodayBacklog
components.css: .ds-nav { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 2px; padding: 4px; border-radius: 999px; background: var(--muted); } .ds-nav a { display: inline-flex; height: 32px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 0 14px; border-radius: 999px; color: var(--muted-foreground); font: 500 0.8125rem/1.125rem "Avenir Next", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link:hover { background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); } .ds-nav-active { background: var(--foreground); color: var(--background) !important; font-weight: 600 !important; } .ds-nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); outline-offset: 0; }

components.name: Task Row
components.description: A quiet, scannable task row with completion and duration signals.
components.html: Review planning brief25m
components.css: .ds-task-row { display: flex; min-height: 56px; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 4px 6px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); color: var(--foreground); font: 400 0.8125rem/1.125rem "Avenir Next", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif; } .ds-checkbox { width: 20px; height: 20px; flex: 0 0 auto; border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: 999px; background: transparent; } .ds-task-title { min-width: 0; flex: 1; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-task-time { flex: 0 0 auto; color: var(--muted-foreground); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-task-row:focus-within { background: var(--muted); } .ds-checkbox:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); outline-offset: 0; }

components.name: Capacity Rail
components.description: A thin commitment signal paired with explicit minutes.
components.css: .ds-capacity { color: var(--foreground); font: 600 0.8125rem/1.125rem "Avenir Next", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif; } .ds-capacity-label { display: flex; align-items: baseline; justify-content: space-between; gap: 16px; } .ds-capacity-label span:last-child { color: var(--muted-foreground); font-size: 0.6875rem; } .ds-capacity-track { height: 4px; margin-top: 8px; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 999px; background: var(--muted); } .ds-capacity-fill { display: block; width: 62%; height: 100%; border-radius: inherit; background: var(--primary); }

narrative.overview:
Slate is a calm, compact planning surface for one decision: what fits today. The interface uses familiar product-UI vocabulary—short labels, clear hierarchy, standard controls, and quiet boundaries—so the planning workflow stays more visible than the interface itself.

The visual system is restrained rather than decorative. Near-neutral surfaces establish a soft desktop canvas, a single muted teal accent marks commitment and state, and serif type creates a brief reflective pause only where the product asks the user to orient. The system explicitly rejects project-management density, calendar/time-blocking metaphors, and AI surfaces that imply control without confirmation.
keyCharacteristics: Compact-first geometry that remains usable in the 360 × 520 minimum window.
Tonal surfaces and hairline boundaries instead of decorative card shadows.
Serif type for orientation and reflection; sans-serif type for every active workflow.
One restrained accent used for commitment, selection, focus, and meaningful status.
State changes communicated through text, shape, and hierarchy as well as color.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Use the primary teal only when it communicates action, progress, selection, focus, or meaningful state. Do not use it as page decoration.

rules.name: The Token Rule
rules.body: Use the semantic CSS variables from src/styles.css. Do not introduce near-matching neutral or accent values in component classes.

rules.name: The Reflection Rule
rules.body: Serif type belongs to high-level orientation and a short reflective moment. Controls, task metadata, and operational labels stay in the sans-serif family.

rules.name: The Quiet Surface Rule
rules.body: Do not combine a visible border with a broad soft shadow on ordinary cards or controls. Use tonal layering and a border for static content; reserve elevation for transient interaction layers.

dos: Do use background, foreground, card, border, muted, primary, and ring as semantic roles from src/styles.css.
Do reserve the teal accent for an explicit task decision, progress, selection, focus, or meaningful state.
Do keep the core workflow understandable at 360 × 520 before optimizing the full window.
Do use standard keyboard-operable controls with visible focus and 150–250ms state transitions.
Do provide empty, completed, over-capacity, persistence-error, loading, and disabled states with concise explanatory copy.
Do keep AI results visually secondary and reviewable until the user explicitly accepts them.
Do use semantic labels and text alongside color for completion, over-capacity, configured, and error states.
donts: Don't make Slate resemble a busy project-management suite through dense dashboards, decorative metrics, or competing accent colors.
Don't introduce calendar grids, time-blocking visual metaphors, or time-of-day scheduling controls into the core daily planning surface.
Don't make AI actions look autonomous; every suggestion must remain distinguishable and require user confirmation.
Don't use gradient text, colored side stripes, decorative glass cards, or broad soft shadows on bordered panels.
Don't use the primary color as page decoration or use a serif face in controls, task data, and operational labels.
Don't use nested cards merely to separate paragraphs or create hierarchy.
Don't rely on tiny low-contrast gray text for essential capacity, error, or persistence information.
